account.loginDesc: After signing in, you can sync your workspace across devices. Share links with invite codes for referral attribution. You can also create anonymously using browser cookies.
account.oauthHint: WeChat / QQ / Feishu / LINE / Douyin require app credentials in .env before they can be enabled. Use Development Login in local development.
account.registerDesc: Register with username and password — no email verification required.
account.registerUsernameHint: 3–32 chars, start with a letter; letters, digits, underscore only
account.registerPlatformTagline: AI novels · comics · long-form production chain

home.strategyTitle: From idea to finished work, in one place
home.strategyDesc: See AI results fast first; when you want to refine, continue writing, storyboarding, and publishing in the same product.
home.literaryPipelineDesc: Not one-off random generation — Brief → chapters → characters → storyboard → final art, all iterating in one project.
home.ctaDesc: From one sentence to something playable, readable, and watchable - your work starts now.

pillars.wowBody: The unified creation entry recommends games, novels, or comics from your idea so you can quickly feel: this is what I wanted.
pillars.depthTitle: Hand off to the creator workspace
pillars.depthBody: If you like it, save and share it. Open the workspace to continue writing, fix panels, and tune settings.
pillars.communityBody: Browse community works and remix popular ideas so consumption becomes the starting point for the next creation.

productIa.headline: Turn one sentence into something playable, readable, and watchable
productIa.subhead: Games, novels, and comics are three independent pipelines. One idea can pick any medium without auto-linking the others.

steps.step1Body: No need to choose a format first. Just describe what you want to see.
steps.step2Body: The system recommends the best medium and gets you something visible as quickly as possible.
steps.step3Body: If you like it, save and share it. If not, switch direction and keep going.

modes.gameTagline: See a playable version fastest, ideal for demos and sharing
modes.novelTagline: Best for stories, chapters, children’s content, and longer serials
modes.comicTagline: Standalone prompts and materials, or adapt from your existing novels
modes.comicEta: About 5-40 minutes depending on panel count

starterPrompts: A hand-drawn grassland tower defense where wooden arrow towers guard a carrot cottage from small animals along a dirt road
A warm children’s short story about a little white rabbit who shares carrots in the forest
A time traveler arrives at Coal Hill in the late Ming era and must change the course of history in seven days
productIa.discoverIntakeTitle: No idea yet? Start by remixing someone else's work
productIa.discoverIntakeBody: Browse community creations, copy a setup into your workspace with one click, and turn it into your own version.

featured.hotSamplesDesc: 23 instant-play demos — chess, 2048, temple runner, and more

literaryChain.title: A production chain you can keep advancing
literaryChain.subtitle: Not one-off random generation — Brief → chapters → character assets → storyboard → comic fixes, all in one project loop.
literaryChain.stepOutlineDesc: Expand a one-liner into Brief, three-act beats and genre — skip the blank-page stall.
literaryChain.stepChaptersDesc: Segmented long-form generation, resume checkpoints and chapter plans for serial work.
literaryChain.stepCharactersDesc: Profiles, relationships and reference sheets persist across chapters and comic panels.
literaryChain.stepStoryboardDesc: Shot logic, dialogue density and page rhythm from novel semantics — not one-shot images.
literaryChain.stepComicDesc: Storyboards stay in-project for review, redraw and serial chapter adaptation.

comicStandaloneChain.title: Comic-native pipeline
comicStandaloneChain.subtitle: No novel binding: pitch → script → storyboard → art, all within this comic project.
comicStandaloneChain.stepPitchDesc: One-line idea, story text, or uploads. Idea-only input is expanded into a comic script.
comicStandaloneChain.stepScriptDesc: Expansion and pacing stay inside the comic project—no novel record, no chapter serial.
comicStandaloneChain.stepStoryboardDesc: Page-level panels, dialogue, and visual prompts; reorder and inline-edit per panel.
comicStandaloneChain.stepArtDesc: Generate, redraw, and fix panels; standalone works can be shared when ready.

comicStandaloneAdvanced.hint: When off, the length preset above applies (~{preset} pages). When on, set page count and panels per page.

characterRoster.title: Consistency profiles & references

start.title: Start with your idea
start.desc: No need to pick a format first. We’ll recommend whether it should become a game, novel, or comic, then take you to the right creation page.
start.pipelineNote: Three independent pipelines: choosing comic won’t create a novel. Linking only happens from a novel page or “From my novel” in comic create.
start.placeholder: For example: a hand-drawn grassland tower defense with arrow towers guarding a carrot cottage while little animals attack along a dirt road...
start.sampleMatchBody: We recommend starting with a game — same engine and polish as the official demo

comicPanel.consistencyBody: From the novel body and creative brief, the system locks character design → generates reference art in parallel → storyboard panels include dialogue/narration overlays (no readable text in the art) → panel art anchors to references and the first panel style. Long works generate storyboard text first, then parallel panel art on the comic page.

smash-the-dummy.subtitle: 物理发泄 · 连击评分 · 夸张反馈
smash-the-dummy.prompt: 做一个解压向物理小游戏：场景里有一个可被打、踢、投掷的 dummy 假人，玩家点击或拖拽武器砸向它，触发连击计数、屏幕震动和夸张粒子；要有多种打击方式、分数与连击 HUD，整体节奏爽快、反馈强。

rail-in-air.subtitle: 空中轨道 · 过山车视角 · 速度冲刺
rail-in-air.prompt: 做一个 3D 空中轨道/过山车竞速小游戏：玩家在立体悬空的轨道上高速前进，可在第一人称与第三人称视角切换；轨道有弯道、爬升、俯冲与跳跃段，强调速度感与离心力反馈，带计时或距离得分 HUD。视觉偏明亮卡通 3D。

grow-a-garden.subtitle: 种植养成 · 浇水收获 · 轻度模拟
grow-a-garden.prompt: 做一个休闲种植小游戏：网格地块上播种、浇水、等待生长并收获；有金币经济、可购买新种子与装饰，UI 清新可爱，带简单教程与进度目标。

color-bloom.subtitle: 色彩消除 · 连锁 bloom · 治愈视觉
color-bloom.prompt: 做一个色彩消除类益智小游戏：点击相邻同色块形成 bloom 连锁，清除后上方方块下落；关卡目标清晰，配色柔和饱和，带连击特效与步数/时间限制。

whimsy-differences.subtitle: 找不同 · 双图对比 · 限时挑战
whimsy-differences.prompt: 做一个找不同小游戏：左右两幅插画仅几处细节不同，玩家点击差异处圈出；有多关卡、倒计时与提示次数，画风 whimsical 手绘，差异难度递进。

gun-merge-3d-zombie-apocalypse.subtitle: 枪械合成 · 塔防射击 · 僵尸潮
gun-merge-3d-zombie-apocalypse.prompt: 做一个 3D 俯视射击+合成游戏：玩家合并同级枪械升级火力，在场地边缘布置或手持射击，抵御多波僵尸；有 merge 网格、波次、金币掉落与基地 HP，氛围偏末日但 Q 版低多边形。

ultimate-3d-chess.subtitle: 3D 棋盘 · 回合策略 · 经典规则
ultimate-3d-chess.prompt: 做一个 3D 视角国际象棋小游戏：标准规则，可点击棋子显示合法走法，带简单 AI 对手、悔一步与将军提示；棋盘与棋子用 stylized 3D 或等距视角，操作清晰。

elastic-thief-2.subtitle: 伸缩潜行 · 物理摆荡 · 偷取目标
elastic-thief-2.prompt: 做一个潜行物理小游戏：玩家控制可伸缩/elastic 的角色摆荡或伸长去偷取场景中的目标物品，避开守卫与激光；关卡短平快，强调物理手感与失败重试。

state-conquest.subtitle: 区域占领 · 数值对抗 · 地图策略
state-conquest.prompt: 做一个地图征服策略小游戏：若干区域节点，玩家与 AI 轮流派兵占领相邻区域，占领全部或达到分数即胜；有兵力数字、动画反馈与难度递增。

tiny-planet-chopper.subtitle: 迷你星球 · 环绕飞行 · 收集斩击
tiny-planet-chopper.prompt: 做一个 tiny planet 环绕飞行小游戏：小星球表面有树木/障碍，直升机或 chopper 环绕星球飞行，玩家点击斩击收集资源并躲避障碍；单指操作，节奏轻快。（原创星球与载具造型）

blade-defender-merge.subtitle: 剑刃合成 · 防线塔防 · 波次升级
blade-defender-merge.prompt: 做一个 merge + 塔防混合游戏：在格子上合并同级 blade/剑塔提升攻击力，自动攻击沿路径前进的敌人；有多波次、金币与技能冷却，视觉偏奇幻 RPG。（原创敌人与剑塔造型）

car-color-palette.subtitle: 汽车涂色 · 调色盘 · 创意定制
car-color-palette.prompt: 做一个汽车涂色定制小游戏：展示一辆 stylized 小车，玩家从调色盘选色填充车身、轮毂、背景；可随机配色、截图分享感 UI，操作简单儿童友好。

blocky-sniper-hunter.subtitle: 方块狙击 · 瞄准射击 · 关卡目标
blocky-sniper-hunter.prompt: 做一个 blocky 低多边形狙击小游戏：第一人称或 over-shoulder 瞄准，射击关卡中的目标物体；有限子弹、部位判定与星级评价，场景像 minecraft 风格方块世界但为原创地图。

memory-match-mania.subtitle: 翻牌配对 · 记忆挑战 · 连击计时
memory-match-mania.prompt: 做一个翻牌记忆配对小游戏：网格背面卡牌，点击翻两张，相同则消除；有步数/计时、连击加分与多主题牌面，UI 明亮活泼。（原创牌面图案）

kids-puzzle.subtitle: 儿童拼图 · 拖拽块 · 简单关卡
kids-puzzle.prompt: 做一个儿童向拼图小游戏：大块拼图拖拽到轮廓位置，完成即过关；图案可爱、块数少、有语音提示位（文字即可）与星星奖励。（原创卡通图案，不用版权 IP）

pottery-master-3d.subtitle: 3D pottery · wheel throwing · glaze customization
pottery-master-3d.prompt: Create a 3D pottery wheel mini-game: wet clay on a spinning wheel grows taller with taps into a vase; pick glaze/rim/base colors from a palette, spin to showcase the finished piece; simple controls, instant feedback, pass after a few color rounds.

crashy-roads.subtitle: Endless road · lane dodge · distance score
crashy-roads.prompt: Create an endless road driving mini-game: car moves forward automatically, player switches lanes with left/right to dodge obstacles and broken-down vehicles; score by distance, lose lives on collision; pseudo-3D perspective road that speeds up over time.

ref.title: Reference assets
ref.summary: PNG/WebP/GIF keep alpha; sticker assets auto-fit sprite cells; set a purpose
ref.summaryTooltip: PNG/WebP/GIF saved to session with <transparent>alpha preserved</transparent> (no white fill). In the clipboard queue, set a <purpose>purpose</purpose> per image: mark as enemy/hero/tower <sticker>sticker types</sticker> to auto <spriteGrid>center-fit square sprite cells</spriteGrid> for tower-defense marches; do not mark large map backgrounds as enemies/towers. <noMatting>Server does not AI-mat</noMatting> complex photos — supply transparent assets yourself. With "Interpret reference images" checked, the model adds practical suggestions per image.
ref.noUploadHint: You can generate without uploads — the platform will draw characters automatically
ref.searchEnhanceHint: On by default; searches similar games and injects findings. Skips if Tavily key is missing
ref.generateHint: Click Generate to auto-parse queued assets; URLs in the link field are fetched and merged too
ref.generateTooltipA: When you click Generate, queued files/clipboard images or a public URL in the link field (e.g. mc.163.com) are parsed and appended to the description first.
ref.generateTooltipB: Upload PDF / DOCX / TXT / MD / images, <paste>Ctrl+V / ⌘V paste screenshots</paste>, or paste a public page link. Session writes <compress>auto-resize/compress</compress> first; if storage limits are hit, quality drops or trailing images are removed automatically. Tower-defense play uses <bg>background-class</bg> assets as maps and <enemy>enemy-class</enemy> as march sprites by purpose.

templateNotes.litellm: Common in enterprises: internal LiteLLM Proxy with /v1 Base URL; model IDs match litellm config model_name. Split text vs image into two providers.
templateNotes.litellm_image: Image-only LiteLLM pool; bind comic_image_openai route; supports gpt-image / DALL·E via OpenAI images API.
templateNotes.oneapi: One API / New API relay; OpenAI-compatible /v1; model names depend on channel config.
templateNotes.openai: Official OpenAI API; default Base URL https://api.openai.com/v1; models like gpt-4o, gpt-4.1, o3-mini.
templateNotes.azure_openai: Azure OpenAI: Base URL includes resource + deployment; model ID is deployment name, not OpenAI model name.
templateNotes.anthropic: Anthropic Messages API; choose Anthropic Messages protocol (not OpenAI-compatible); fixed api.anthropic.com endpoint.
templateNotes.gemini: Google Gemini REST; text and image; enterprise proxy can replace Base URL.
templateNotes.mistral: Mistral official OpenAI-compatible /v1; models like mistral-large-latest.
templateNotes.groq: Groq fast inference, OpenAI-compatible; good for low-cost fallback.
templateNotes.xai: xAI Grok OpenAI-compatible endpoint; models like grok-2-latest.
templateNotes.openrouter: OpenRouter unified routing; model IDs use vendor prefix e.g. openai/gpt-4o; create Key in OpenRouter console.
templateNotes.together: Together AI hosted open models; IDs often org/model format.
templateNotes.fireworks: Fireworks AI inference; OpenAI-compatible; see console for model IDs.
templateNotes.moonshot: Moonshot Kimi, OpenAI-compatible; moonshot-v1-8k etc.; watch RPM and context limits.
templateNotes.dashscope: Alibaba DashScope OpenAI-compatible mode; Qwen qwen-max / qwen-plus; Key from Model Studio.
templateNotes.zhipu: Zhipu OpenAI-compatible v4 path; GLM-4 series; check bigmodel.cn Key format.
templateNotes.doubao: Volcengine Ark Doubao, OpenAI-compatible /api/v3; model ID is endpoint inference ID.
templateNotes.siliconflow: SiliconFlow aggregates CN open models; IDs use HuggingFace-style paths.
templateNotes.custom: Fully custom: set vendor name, protocol, Base URL, and model list yourself.

prep.header: ── How the system will handle your prompt (summary, not hidden chain-of-thought):
prep.searchOn: **Web search enabled** — fetch similar play/style cues first, then merge summaries into the brief.
prep.searchOff: **Web search off** this round — text plus optional reference excerpts only.
prep.enhanceOn: Running a **second enhancement pass** to thicken systems and readable numbers.
prep.enhanceOff: Enhancement pass off — single spec pass, then local repair/validation.

fantasy.cute: Cute characters / relaxed mood / cozy tone
fantasy.default: A unified world built from your current description

gameplayCore.towerDefense: Path marching, tower upgrades, elite waves, and hold-the-point pressure together.
gameplayCore.shooter: Move-dodge, auto fire, enemy formations, and short-cooldown skills in a loop.
gameplayCore.platformer: Jump feel, terrain, collect goals, and mechanic shifts drive progress.
gameplayCore.collector: Move-collect and avoid threats in parallel for steady reward feedback.
gameplayCore.survivor: Forgiveness and pressure together — survival decisions get harder over time.
gameplayCore.avoider: Short dodge rounds and clear goals — avoid random obstacle soup.

intent.strengthEnough: One sentence is enough for a first GameSpec — refine via direction picks.
intent.riskGoal: The win condition is still vague — add what the player must ultimately do.
intent.riskThreat: Threats are vague — you may get theme without pressure.
intent.riskProgression: Progression cues are thin — use direction picks to add layers.

directionBullets.commonLead: Keep title, enemies, goals, and HUD naming unified under 「{fantasy}」.
directionBullets.td0: Stress three tower roles, elite waves, and hold events.
directionBullets.shooter0: Stress formations, fire windows, and burst skills.
directionBullets.platformer0: Level beats, terrain mechanics, timed micro-goals.
directionBullets.platformer1: Forward exploration, not random single-screen jumps.
directionBullets.default0: Goals, threats, and phase shifts — not theme-only wrapping.
directionBullets.depthExtra: Thicker mid-layer decisions and phase shifts.
directionBullets.spectacleExtra1: Stronger titles, subtitles, faction naming, and climax beats.
directionBullets.spectacleExtra2: Still playable — bolder thematic packaging allowed.

balanced.title: Ship-ready
balanced.summary: Clear rules, obvious goals, easy first play.
balanced.addonLine2: Goals, fail states, and progression visible at a glance.
balanced.addonLine3: Conservative numbers for a stable v1.

depth.title: Deeper systems
depth.summary: More events, skills, phases, and mid-layer decisions.
depth.addonLine2: Boost at least two of: skills, events, elites/bosses, goal shifts.
depth.addonLine3: Not just reskin — visibly richer gameplay.

spectacle.title: Spectacle first
spectacle.summary: Strong theme, naming, and climax while staying playable.
spectacle.addonLine2: Sharper theme naming, HUD copy, and set-piece beats.
spectacle.addonLine3: Memorable world on top of a complete loop.

classic.title: Classic arcade shmup?
classic.summary: Auto-fire upward, dense enemy waves — dodging patterns is the core.
classic.bullet0: Enemy formations + escalating waves within ~3 minutes
classic.bullet1: Player = fighter jet; foes = enemy craft; drops = coins or energy cores
classic.addon0: shooter template: auto-fire, wave spawns, vertical/top-down air combat
classic.addon1: Bullet density + dodge windows; a clear beat every 20–40s

boss.title: Add a mothership / boss phase?
boss.summary: Clear fodder first, then a multi-phase boss (shields → core).
boss.bullet0: At least one multi-phase boss with pattern shifts
boss.bullet1: Win by destroying the core or holding the line
boss.addon0: shooter + phased boss: shields, core, add spawns
boss.addon1: Elite wave before final boss with dedicated HUD callout
boss.addon2: Theme “{fantasy}” in boss name and stage captions

power.title: Power-up snowball loop?
power.summary: Enemies drop upgrades — fire rate, spread, shields stack for payoff.
power.bullet1: Combos or kill streaks amplify feedback
power.addon0: shooter: visible power growth from enemy drops
power.addon1: Shift skill vs pickup skills have distinct roles
power.addon2: Tuned for “getting stronger” in the first session

story.title: Lean into warzone story?
story.summary: Named sectors, enemy legions, mission briefs — still fully playable.
story.bullet1: subtitle = mood; HUD goals = actionable rules
story.addon0: Air combat shooter + distinct factions and chapter names
story.addon1: labels: player=fighter, hazard=enemy craft, collectible=energy core
story.addon2: Long lore in subtitle/chapters; keep top HUD short

lane.title: Single-lane classic TD?
lane.summary: One path, multiple tower slots, readable economy and waves.
lane.bullet0: Three tower roles (DPS / AoE / slow or buff)
lane.bullet1: Clear wave table; leaks damage the base
lane.addon0: towerDefense: one path, fixed slots, rising waves
lane.addon1: Economy: afford 2–3 towers early; elites mid-game

multi.title: Split paths to divide pressure?
multi.bullet0: Fast near lane, tanky far lane
multi.addon0: Multiple paths merging at choke points
multi.addon1: At least one “both lanes spike” beat

hero.title: Add a movable hero?
hero.summary: Towers hold zones; hero plugs leaks and bursts with skills.
hero.bullet0: Hero cooldown skill (clear / slow / heal base)
hero.bullet1: Tower upgrades + hero growth in parallel
hero.addon1: Shift = hero skill; balanced with build economy

events.title: Random wave events?
events.summary: Elites, air drops, terrain shifts — force plan changes.

run.title: Run-and-collect stages?
run.summary: Move forward, grab coins/items, reach goal or quota.

precision.title: Tight precision jumps?
precision.summary: Narrow landings, fast rhythm, light penalty but real pressure.
precision.addon0: Jump feel first; short beats, fast feedback

combat.title: Combat on platforms?
combat.summary: Jump plus stomp/fight enemies — not just hazards.
combat.bullet1: Defeats drop items or open gates

chapters.title: Chapter-based story?
chapters.summary: Per-chapter goals, palette shifts, chapter titles.
chapters.bullet1: Chapter name vs subtitle roles (mood vs rules)
chapters.addon1: Short HUD goals; long copy not in top bar

goal.title: How should the player win?
goal.summary: Pin the win condition — collect, hold, reach, or defeat a boss.
goal.bullet0: Make winScore / stage goal / time limit explicit
goal.addon1: Goal panel uses short rule text

threat.title: Where does pressure come from?
threat.summary: Pick the main threat: chasers, bullets, traps, leaks, or scarcity.
threat.addon1: Themed foes — not faceless random blocks

progression.title: How does it escalate?
progression.summary: Waves, skills, chapters, or events that layer pressure.
progression.addon0: Use at least two of: waves, chapters, skills, events
progression.addon1: Avoid one flat rhythm all match

fantasy.title: Deepen this world direction?
fantasy.summary: Unified naming, palette, and item/enemy terms — one coherent world.
fantasy.bullet0: Title, subtitle, HUD, enemies share vocabulary
fantasy.bullet1: Playable loop first; memorable theme second
fantasy.addon1: Long mood in subtitle; short goals on HUD
fantasy.addon2: Sprite prompts match theme (fighters, not random cute blocks)

ingestMarkers.imageNumberLegend: [Ref image numbering] Below, "Image 1", "Image 2", … follow the upload order for images only (aligned with "Ref image #N"; PDF/plain text are not counted). When generating the spec, apply each image's stated purpose to title/subtitle, theme colors, and labels (player/hazard/collectible): palette and mood must match each image excerpt and purpose. For tower-defense templates, after a successful parse the browser play session in the same tab uses reference pixels as sprites (background maps → fullscreen backdrop, monsters → marching foes, hero/carrot/crystal → path goals); JSON theme/hazard/player hues and names must not default to neon/cyber unless the brief or references call for it. Browser-side tweaks (with vision captions): PNG/WebP/GIF keep alpha; sprite-like roles are auto-centered in square cells; the server does not auto-mat complex photos—use transparent PNGs or DCC tools when needed.
